tools.strengths: Provides a fast, probabilistic metric for assessing protein-protein interface quality.
tools.weaknesses: Heuristic score; does not simulate physical kinetics or folding thermodynamics.
tools.github_description: Pinc: a simple probabilistic AlphaFold interaction score

tools.name: OncoPepDesign
tools.usage: Target-agnostic pipeline designing mutant-selective peptides combining RFdiffusion generation with Boltz-2 folding.
tools.strengths: Integrates multiple scoring tools (DeepPpIScore, HADDOCK, MM-GBSA) for consensus selection.
tools.weaknesses: Computationally expensive; relies heavily on accurate co-folding predictions.
tools.github_description: Target-agnostic pipeline for designing peptides selective for oncogenic mutant proteins over wild-type.

tools.name: Boltz-2
tools.github_description: Official repository for the Boltz biomolecular interaction models
tools.preprint_full_citation: Passaro, S. et al., (2025), 'Boltz-2: Towards Accurate and Efficient Binding Affinity Prediction', *bioRxiv*.
tools.usage: Permissively MIT-licensed model designed to jointly predict biomolecular complex structures and estimate binding affinities.
tools.strengths: High structure accuracy; predicts binding affinities in a single GPU-accelerated step; approaches FEP accuracy at 1000x speedup.
tools.weaknesses: Requires project-specific experimental data fine-tuning for high-resolution lead optimization.

tools.name: BoltzProt-1
tools.preprint_full_citation: Uçar, T. et al., (2026), 'BoltzProt-1: Towards Efficient De Novo Binder Design with Good Developability', *bioRxiv*.
tools.usage: Pipeline designed for the de novo generation of protein binders, including VHH nanobodies, focusing on hit rate and developability.
tools.strengths: High developability profiles (stability, purity, aggregation resistance); integrates interaction confidence scoring.
tools.weaknesses: Proprietary API and platform access, not open source.

tools.name: BoltzPPI
tools.preprint_full_citation: Uçar, T. et al., (2026), 'BoltzProt-1: Towards Efficient De Novo Binder Design with Good Developability', *bioRxiv*.
tools.usage: Protein-protein interaction prediction model specifically trained on structural and patent data to score binding interface confidence.
tools.strengths: Evaluates candidates based on binding confidence rather than just structural stability; increases wet-lab success rates.
tools.weaknesses: Part of proprietary BoltzProt-1 pipeline.

tools.name: BoltzMol-1
tools.usage: Generative hit-discovery and high-throughput virtual screening pipeline for small molecules.
tools.strengths: Filters candidate compounds for synthesizability and ADME properties; utilizes Boltz-2 affinity estimation.
tools.weaknesses: Proprietary API and platform access.

tools.name: ProChoreo
tools.github_description: ProChoreo: de novo Binder Design from Conformational Ensembles with Generative Deep Learning
tools.preprint_full_citation: Ding, S. and Zhang, Y. (2026), 'ProChoreo: de novo Binder Design from Conformational Ensembles with Generative Deep Learning', *bioRxiv*.
tools.usage: Deep learning framework for de novo binder design that aligns sequences with MD-derived target conformational ensembles.
tools.strengths: Designs binders targeting receptor dynamics and structural flexibility rather than static structures.
tools.weaknesses: Dependent on computationally heavy MD simulations for target receptor ensembles.

tools.name: Emyx
tools.preprint_full_citation: Emyx, (2026), 'Emyx: Fast and efficient all-atom protein generation', *arXiv*.
tools.usage: 140M-parameter conditional flow matching model for fast all-atom enzyme scaffold generation.
tools.strengths: Low training compute cost (~682 GPU-hours); generates scaffolds around catalytic residues and ligands.
tools.weaknesses: Relies on sparse geometric constraints; limited co-evolutionary context integration.

tools.name: TeSS
tools.github_description: ELIXIR Training e-Support System (TeSS)
tools.full_citation: Beard, N. et al., (2020), 'TeSS: a platform for discovering life science training opportunities', *Bioinformatics*, 36(10), 3292–3293.
tools.usage: ELIXIR Training e-Support Service (TeSS), a portal aggregating life science training events, materials, and learning workflows.
tools.strengths: Comprehensive aggregation of ELIXIR and community training resources; supports widgets and open programmatic API access.
tools.weaknesses: Acts as an aggregator registry rather than hosting training content directly.

social_posts.text: Alphafoldology is moving fast! Just reviewed RoseTTAFold-All-Atom. Predicting small molecules, RNA/DNA, and modified proteins all in one open framework. A massive leap for structural biology.
Need to search millions of AlphaFold structures? Foldseek does structural alignment in milliseconds. Essential tool for the downstream #alphafoldology research pipeline.
ColabFold has reached a huge milestone in accelerating structural biology. By pairing MMseqs2 fast search with AF2, we've enabled researchers worldwide to predict structures in minutes.
Check out the upcoming 'Advanced in silico Drug Design' workshop in Olomouc! Karel Berka will lead lectures on #alphafoldology and practical downstream analysis for structural biology.
